theory.intro: When outcomes are equally likely, the **probability** of an event is the fraction of outcomes that give that event.
facts: $P(\text{event}) = \dfrac{\text{number of favourable outcomes}}{\text{total number of outcomes}}$.
A probability is always between $0$ (impossible) and $1$ (certain).
For a fair die, $P(4) = \tfrac{1}{6}$ because $1$ of the $6$ faces is a $4$.
steps: Count the total number of equally likely outcomes.
Count how many of them are favourable to the event.
Write the probability as favourable over total, then simplify if you can.

foundation.q: What is the probability of spinning red on this fair spinner?
A fair coin is tossed. What is the probability of heads?
A fair die is rolled. What is the probability of rolling a $5$?
What is the probability of spinning a $2$ on this fair spinner?
A bag has $1$ red, $1$ blue and $1$ green marble (all identical apart from colour). What is $P(\text{blue})$?
A fair die is rolled. What is the probability of rolling an even number?
What is the probability of spinning B on this fair $6$-sector spinner?
Cards numbered $1$ to $4$ (identical backs) are shuffled and one drawn. What is $P(3)$?
A fair die is rolled. What is the probability of rolling a number less than $3$?
What is the probability of spinning win on this fair spinner?

development.q: This fair $8$-sector spinner is numbered $1$ to $8$. What is the probability of spinning an even number?
A bag holds $3$ red and $2$ blue counters (identical otherwise). What is $P(\text{red})$?
A die is rolled. What is the probability of rolling a number greater than $4$?
A spinner has $4$ sectors: $2$ red, $1$ blue, $1$ green (equal sizes). What is $P(\text{red})$?
A bag holds counters numbered $1$ to $10$ (identical otherwise). What is the probability of drawing a multiple of $3$?
A fair die is rolled. What is the probability of rolling a $7$?
A letter is chosen at random from the word **BANANA**. What is $P(\text{A})$?
A spinner has $5$ equal sectors numbered $1$ to $5$. What is the probability of an odd number?
A fair die is rolled. What is the probability of rolling a number from $1$ to $6$?
A bag has $4$ red, $3$ blue and $1$ yellow counter. What is $P(\text{blue})$?

mastery.q: A bag has $6$ counters: $2$ red, $1$ blue, $3$ green. Find $P(\text{not green})$.
A spinner of $8$ equal sectors is numbered $1$ to $8$. Find $P(\text{a factor of } 8)$.
A letter is chosen from **PROBABILITY**. Find $P(\text{a vowel})$.
A die is rolled. Find $P(\text{prime number})$.
A bag has red and blue counters only. If $P(\text{red}) = \tfrac{2}{5}$ and there are $10$ counters, how many are blue?
A spinner has $4$ equal sectors. To make $P(\text{red}) = \tfrac{3}{4}$, how many sectors must be red?
Two coins are tossed. Find $P(\text{exactly one head})$.
A bag has $12$ marbles, $4$ of them striped. Find $P(\text{not striped})$.
A die is rolled. Find $P(\text{a square number})$.
A spinner of $10$ equal sectors is numbered $1$ to $10$. Find $P(\text{a two-digit number})$.
